# agent-onboard

agent-onboard is the public npm package for AI-assisted repository onboarding, target-repo orientation, work-item coordination, and release-safe handoff.

Target:

- name: agent-onboard
- kind: node
- control package: agent-onboard; version source: package.json#version

Canonical AI-readable files:

- AGENTS.md
- SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md
- .agent-onboard/authority-path.json
- llms.txt
- package.json
- authority-map.json
- manifest.json
- .agent-onboard/work-items.json
- README.md

Stable commands:

- agent-onboard discovery --llms
- agent-onboard create --dry-run
- agent-onboard issue --classify-dry-run --text
- agent-onboard contributor --admission-dry-run --text
- agent-onboard check --plan --text
- agent-onboard check --fast --text
- agent-onboard ci --github-action
- agent-onboard mcp --plan --text
- agent-onboard discovery --text
- agent-onboard discovery --json
- agent-onboard guide --text
- agent-onboard quickstart --text
- agent-onboard commands --text
- agent-onboard target doctor --text
- agent-onboard target memory --text
- agent-onboard target work-items --text
- agent-onboard target governance --text
- agent-onboard target governance --budget-contract --text
- agent-onboard target governance --budget-check --text
- agent-onboard target governance --materialize-dry-run --text
- agent-onboard target governance --check --text
- agent-onboard check --fast --text
- agent-onboard target handoff --text
- agent-onboard target handoff --readiness-check --text
- agent-onboard contracts --text
- agent-onboard contracts --check --json
- agent-onboard contracts --validate-output --contract <id> --file <path> --json
- agent-onboard target onboarding --plan
- agent-onboard work-items --next --text
- agent-onboard release --source-manifest
- agent-onboard release --check

Default safety:

- Start read-only.
- Do not write files, install dependencies, publish, push, mutate Git, run managed project commands, or use network calls unless the repository owner explicitly authorizes that action.
- Use explicit --write commands only after previewing the plan and receiving owner authorization.

First-read order:

1. AGENTS.md — human and agent operating rules.
2. SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md — human-readable authority precedence.
3. .agent-onboard/authority-path.json — machine-readable authority path index.
4. llms.txt — AI-readable command and orientation entrypoint.
5. package.json — package identity, scripts, and pack surface.
6. authority-map.json — stable file URN and authority registry.
7. manifest.json — content identity and file coverage index.
8. .agent-onboard/target.json — target boundary declaration.
9. .agent-onboard/runtime-namespace.json — target runtime namespace declaration.
10. .agent-onboard/project.json — target runtime identity.
11. .agent-onboard/work-items.json — public work item ledger.
12. README.md — public package or repository documentation.
13. raw evidence/source files — on demand only after the authority and scope files above.

Source of truth order for agents: follow the First-read order above before raw evidence/source files. Optional source-manifest hash cache state is acceleration only; actual filesystem or package projection remains authority for existence.

Use `agent-onboard discovery --llms` to print this AI-readable entrypoint from either the source repository or an installed package. Use `agent-onboard target memory --text` for a metadata-only target memory surface preview before reading raw repo instruction files. Use `agent-onboard target work-items --text` to preview target-owned work-item state without admission, closure, ledger writes, or synthetic next-id generation. Use `agent-onboard target governance --text` to preview compact work-items/claims indexes without writing indexes, inlining raw growth files, admitting claims, or granting authority. Use `agent-onboard target governance --budget-contract --text` to read the stable no-scan first-read cache and budget policy before trusting or materializing compact indexes. Use `agent-onboard target governance --budget-check --text` to validate target index byte budgets without inlining planned payloads. Use `agent-onboard target governance --materialize-dry-run --text` to plan exact compact index payloads and budget impact without writing target state. Use `agent-onboard target governance --check --text` to compare stored governance indexes with freshly derived payloads and report `fresh`, `stale`, or `missing` without writing files. Use `agent-onboard check --fast --text` to see the same stale-read risk as a fast-check advisory. Use `agent-onboard target governance --materialize --write --force --text` only after explicit owner authorization to write allowlisted first-read governance indexes with compare-before-write. Canonical `agent-onboard work-items --init|--append|--claim|--close --write` responses refresh those same indexes after successful admitted work-items mutation and expose the embedded `governance_index_refresh` result. Use `agent-onboard target handoff --text` to compose a compact read-only next-session handoff, including governance budget state, governance index drift state, and structured readiness reason codes, without importing file contents or granting authority. Use `agent-onboard target handoff --readiness-check --text` when an agent or CI needs a compact pass/fail readiness gate over those reason codes without parsing the full handoff preview. Use `agent-onboard contracts --text` or `agent-onboard contracts --check --json` to inspect and validate the compact public contract/interface spine for stable JSON outputs without exporting source-only implementation archives, requiring TypeScript/abstract classes, writing files, or mutating target state. Use `agent-onboard contracts --validate-output --contract <id> --file <path> --json` to validate captured JSON output files without re-emitting file contents or writing state. Use `agent-onboard issue --classify-dry-run --text` to classify external issue metadata before any work-item admission or platform mutation. Use `agent-onboard contributor --admission-dry-run --text` to preview contributor identity, provenance, and AI-assistance attribution before any contributor ledger write or claim admission. Use `agent-onboard ci --github-action` to print a copyable GitHub Actions recipe without creating workflow files or treating CI as authority. Use `agent-onboard mcp --plan --text` to preview a read-only future agent-client/MCP bridge contract without starting a server, opening sockets, adding dependencies, writing files, or treating MCP output as authority.
